title: rubus thibetianus
intro: Rubus thibetianus, also known as the Tibetan raspberry or Tibetan bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus thibetianus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against cardiovascular disease due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and phenolic compounds. Rubus thibetianus can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are commonly used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
